title: orchis sambucina subvar. purpurea
intro: Orchis sambucina subvar. purpurea, also known as the purple orchid or lady's fingers, is a subvariety of the orchid sambucina plant, belonging to the Orchidaceae family. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds. It has been reported to provide several health benefits, such as treating skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and antimicrobial activities. In herbal preparations, Orchis sambucina subvar. purpurea is often used to make infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be applied topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
